entries.claim: Cloud restore is refused when the opt-in flag is off (default).
Local-file restore recovers the exact archived bytes with no token and no network.
A crash after the Graph POST but before the local record does not duplicate the item; recovery reconciles to exactly one.
entries.evidence: C4 crash after POST, before record       Reconciled       1        1  OK  ||  ADR-001 invariant holds: exactly one cloud item in every case, no duplicates.

entries.claim: A concurrent identical restore is rejected by the ledger's unique idempotency key, so only one item is created.
The mass-delete guard halts an over-threshold deletion in both directions.
An interrupted chunked upload resumes from persisted session state across a process kill.
The content-derived idempotency key is deterministic and per-field sensitive.
Mail restore is wired through the ledger: a crash after the POST landed reconciles without creating a duplicate (proven against a non-idempotent fake mailbox).
entries.notes: Live confirmation that Graph returns a posted Message-ID via an internetMessageId $filter is still pending a write token; cloud_restore_enabled stays false until then.

entries.claim: The daemon recovers an in-flight mail restore on boot, reconciling without a new create.
Live: Microsoft Graph preserves a posted Message-ID as internetMessageId and an internetMessageId $filter finds it — the marker probe the crash recovery relies on.
entries.evidence: POST /me/messages -> 201; internetMessageId == posted Message-ID; GET $filter -> 1 match; cleanup DELETE -> 204; VERDICT YES

entries.claim: A non-ledger (non-mail) cloud restore is refused before any store access or token lookup, never run via a non-crash-safe direct POST.
